ReadRangeX.note: Self-closing tag. Modern Excel Read Range (used inside ExcelApplicationCard scope). (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
ReadRangeX.description: StudioX 'Read Range' - read a worksheet range into a DataTable using the active Excel scope.
ReadRangeX.use_when: User wants to read a sheet/range into a DataTable in a modern (StudioX) Excel project. For classic projects use ExcelReadRange; for a single cell use ReadCellValueX or ExcelReadCell.

alternatives.use_instead_when: You are inside a classic ExcelApplicationScope/Workbook scope rather than a modern Use Excel File context.
You only need the value of one cell; ReadRangeX is for rectangular ranges.
You want every cell in one column without specifying the row count.

WriteRangeX.note: Self-closing tag. Modern Excel Write Range (used inside ExcelApplicationCard scope). (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
WriteRangeX.description: StudioX 'Write Range' - write a DataTable into a worksheet range using the active Excel scope.
WriteRangeX.use_when: User wants to push a DataTable into a sheet in StudioX. For appending instead of overwriting use AppendRangeX; for classic projects use ExcelWriteRange.

alternatives.use_instead_when: You are using the classic ExcelApplicationScope/Workbook scope, not a modern Use Excel File scope.
You are writing a single cell value rather than a DataTable.
You want to append below the existing data instead of overwriting from the start cell.

AutoFillX.note: Self-closing tag. Auto-fills a range in an Excel workbook. (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
AutoFillX.description: StudioX 'Auto Fill Range' - extend a formula or value across a destination range using Excel's auto-fill.
AutoFillX.use_when: User wants to drag-fill a formula/value across cells. For row-based fill use FillRangeX; for one-off writes use WriteRangeX.

FilterX.note: Self-closing tag. Applies a filter to an Excel range. (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
FilterX.description: StudioX 'Filter' - apply or clear an AutoFilter on a sheet range or table.
FilterX.use_when: User wants AutoFilter applied to a range/table. For PivotTable filtering use FilterPivotTableX; for classic projects use ExcelFilterTable.

FilterPivotTableX.note: Self-closing tag. Filters a pivot table in Excel. (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
FilterPivotTableX.description: StudioX 'Filter Pivot Table' - apply or clear filters on a PivotTable's pivot fields.
FilterPivotTableX.use_when: User wants to filter a PivotTable. For ordinary range/table filtering use FilterX.

InsertExcelChartX.note: Self-closing tag. Inserts a chart into an Excel worksheet. (dispatched via gen_from_annotation; element_tag drives emission)
InsertExcelChartX.description: StudioX 'Insert Chart' - insert a chart of a chosen type bound to a sheet range.
InsertExcelChartX.use_when: User wants to add a new chart in a StudioX Excel project. To modify an existing chart use UpdateChartX (wizard-only).

ExcelApplicationCard.note: ExcelApplicationCard is a scope activity with ActivityAction body requiring Studio configuration to open/attach to workbooks.
ExcelApplicationCard.description: Modern Use Excel File scope (wizard-only) - opens a workbook and runs nested Excel activities against it.
ExcelApplicationCard.use_when: Do not auto-generate; direct the user to configure Excel Application Card through UiPath Studio's Excel wizard. As an alternative use ExcelApplicationScope (classic) for non-StudioX projects.

alternatives.use_instead_when: You are using classic (non-modern-design) activities and just need a workbook scope around them.
You need a process-wide configuration covering multiple workbooks rather than a card around one Excel session.

ExcelProcessScopeX.note: ExcelProcessScopeX is a scope activity controlling an Excel process instance; requires Studio configuration.
ExcelProcessScopeX.description: Modern Excel Process Scope (wizard-only) - controls a single Excel process instance for nested activities.
ExcelProcessScopeX.use_when: Do not auto-generate; direct the user to configure Excel Process Scope X through UiPath Studio's Excel wizard.

alternatives.use_instead_when: You only need to open and operate on a single workbook with classic activities, not configure a process-wide Excel runtime.
You are authoring inside the Modern Design Application Card pattern rather than a process-level scope.

ExcelForEachRowX.note: ExcelForEachRowX uses a dual-argument ActivityAction body (CurrentRow, CurrentIndex) requiring Studio configuration.
ExcelForEachRowX.description: StudioX For Each Row in Excel (wizard-only) - iterates over rows in a sheet/table with a CurrentRow + CurrentIndex body.
ExcelForEachRowX.use_when: Do not auto-generate; direct the user to author Excel For Each Row X through StudioX. For DataTable iteration use ForEachRow; for sheet iteration use ForEachSheetX (also wizard-only).

ForEachSheetX.note: ForEachSheetX uses a dual-argument ActivityAction body (CurrentSheet, CurrentIndex) requiring Studio configuration.
ForEachSheetX.description: StudioX For Each Sheet (wizard-only) - iterates over each worksheet of the workbook with a CurrentSheet + CurrentIndex body.
ForEachSheetX.use_when: Do not auto-generate; direct the user to author For Each Sheet X through StudioX. For row-level iteration use ExcelForEachRowX (also wizard-only).

UpdateChartX.note: UpdateChartX wraps an ActivityAction body for chart update operations requiring Studio configuration.
UpdateChartX.description: StudioX 'Update Chart' (wizard-only) - updates the data range or properties of an existing chart.
UpdateChartX.use_when: Do not auto-generate; direct the user to author Update Chart X through StudioX. To insert a new chart use InsertExcelChartX.

ChangeDataRangeModification.note: ChangeDataRangeModification has no resolvable CLR namespace and cannot be emitted without Studio wizard.
ChangeDataRangeModification.description: Internal Excel range-modification helper without a stable XAML namespace (wizard-only).
ChangeDataRangeModification.use_when: Do not auto-generate; this activity has no resolvable namespace and must come from Studio's wizard.

AddSensitivityLabelX.description: StudioX activity that applies a Microsoft 365 sensitivity label to the active workbook.
AddSensitivityLabelX.use_when: User wants to set/replace a sensitivity label on a workbook. To read the current label use GetSensitivityLabelX.

AppendCsvFile.description: Append rows from a DataTable to an existing CSV file (or create the file with headers).
AppendCsvFile.use_when: User wants to grow a CSV without rewriting it. To overwrite use WriteCsvFile.

AppendRangeX.description: StudioX 'Append Range' - append a DataTable to the end of an existing sheet/table in the active Excel scope.
AppendRangeX.use_when: User wants to add rows below existing data without overwriting. To overwrite from row 1 use WriteRangeX.

alternatives.use_instead_when: You are using the classic ExcelApplicationScope rather than a modern Use Excel File scope.
The destination is empty (or you want to overwrite) rather than append below existing rows.

AutoFitX.description: StudioX 'Auto Fit' - auto-size columns or rows in a sheet or range.
AutoFitX.use_when: User wants Excel to auto-size widths after writing data.

ChangePivotTableDataSourceX.description: StudioX activity that re-points a PivotTable to a different source range.
ChangePivotTableDataSourceX.use_when: User wants to redirect a PivotTable's data source. To refresh after underlying data changed use RefreshPivotTableX.

ClearRangeX.description: StudioX 'Clear Range' - clear contents and/or formatting from a worksheet range.
ClearRangeX.use_when: User wants to wipe values from cells without deleting the rows/columns. To remove rows use DeleteRowsX.

CloseWorkbook.description: Close an open workbook in the current Excel scope, optionally saving first.
CloseWorkbook.use_when: User wants to close a workbook explicitly. For modern projects this is rarely needed - the Excel scope auto-closes.

CopyChartToClipboardX.description: StudioX activity that copies an Excel chart image to the system clipboard.
CopyChartToClipboardX.use_when: User wants the chart in clipboard form (e.g. to paste into a slide deck or email).

CopyPasteRangeX.description: StudioX 'Copy/Paste Range' - copy a source range and paste it to a destination, with paste-special options.
CopyPasteRangeX.use_when: User wants to duplicate a range with native Excel paste semantics. For classic projects use ExcelCopyPasteRange.

CreateNewWorkbook.description: Create a new empty workbook on disk at the given path.
CreateNewWorkbook.use_when: User wants to scaffold a new .xlsx file before writing data. To open an existing workbook use OpenWorkbook.

CreatePivotTable.description: Create a PivotTable in the workbook (classic Excel API) bound to a source range.
CreatePivotTable.use_when: User wants a PivotTable in classic projects. In StudioX use CreatePivotTableX or CreatePivotTableXv2.

CreatePivotTableX.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

CreatePivotTableX.description: StudioX 'Create Pivot Table' - create a PivotTable bound to a sheet/table range.
CreatePivotTableX.use_when: User wants a PivotTable in StudioX. The newer variant CreatePivotTableXv2 supports additional layout options.

CreatePivotTableXv2.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

CreatePivotTableXv2.description: StudioX 'Create Pivot Table (v2)' - newer variant with additional row/column/value field configuration.
CreatePivotTableXv2.use_when: User wants the newer PivotTable creator with explicit field layout. Otherwise CreatePivotTableX works.

CreateTableX.description: StudioX 'Create Table' - convert a sheet range into a named Excel table (ListObject).
CreateTableX.use_when: User wants a structured named table over a range so other StudioX activities can reference it by name.

DeleteColumnX.description: StudioX 'Delete Column' - remove one or more columns from a sheet or table.
DeleteColumnX.use_when: User wants to drop columns. To clear cell values without deleting columns use ClearRangeX.

DeleteRowsX.description: StudioX 'Delete Rows' - remove one or more rows from a sheet or table.
DeleteRowsX.use_when: User wants to drop rows. To clear cell values without deleting rows use ClearRangeX.

DeleteSheetX.description: StudioX 'Delete Sheet' - delete a worksheet from the active workbook.
DeleteSheetX.use_when: User wants to remove a worksheet. To rename use RenameSheetX; to add use InsertSheetX.

DuplicateSheetX.description: StudioX 'Duplicate Sheet' - copy an existing worksheet to a new sheet in the same workbook.
DuplicateSheetX.use_when: User wants a copy of an existing sheet (template duplication). To insert a blank sheet use InsertSheetX.

ExcelAppendRange.description: Classic 'Append Range' - append a DataTable to an existing sheet inside an ExcelApplicationScope.
ExcelAppendRange.use_when: User wants to append rows in classic Excel projects. For StudioX use AppendRangeX.

ExcelApplicationScope.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

ExcelApplicationScope.description: Classic Excel Application Scope - open a workbook and run nested classic Excel activities against it.
ExcelApplicationScope.use_when: User wants the classic Excel container with explicit Open/Save semantics. For StudioX use ExcelApplicationCard (wizard-only).

alternatives.use_instead_when: The workflow runs the modern (X) Excel activities and you want a process-level scope that covers multiple workbooks rather than per-file.
You are authoring inside a Modern Design experience (Application Card) rather than the classic scope.

ExcelAutoFillRange.description: Classic 'Auto Fill Range' - extend a formula or value across a destination range.
ExcelAutoFillRange.use_when: User wants drag-fill in a classic project. For StudioX use AutoFillX.

ExcelCloseWorkbook.description: Classic 'Close Workbook' - close the workbook of the surrounding ExcelApplicationScope.
ExcelCloseWorkbook.use_when: User wants explicit close semantics in classic projects.

ExcelCopyPasteRange.description: Classic 'Copy/Paste Range' - copy a range to a destination with paste-special options.
ExcelCopyPasteRange.use_when: User wants copy-paste in a classic project. For StudioX use CopyPasteRangeX.

ExcelCopySheet.description: Classic 'Copy Sheet' - copy a worksheet to another workbook or position.
ExcelCopySheet.use_when: User wants to clone a sheet in classic projects. For StudioX use DuplicateSheetX.

ExcelCreatePivotTable.description: Classic 'Create Pivot Table' - create a PivotTable bound to a source range.
ExcelCreatePivotTable.use_when: User wants a PivotTable in classic projects. For StudioX use CreatePivotTableX or CreatePivotTableXv2.

ExcelCreateTable.description: Classic 'Create Table' - convert a range into a named Excel table.
ExcelCreateTable.use_when: User wants a structured table in classic projects. For StudioX use CreateTableX.

ExcelDeleteColumn.description: Classic 'Delete Column' - remove a column from a sheet.
ExcelDeleteColumn.use_when: User wants to drop columns in classic projects. For StudioX use DeleteColumnX.

ExcelDeleteRange.description: Classic 'Delete Range' - delete a range of cells, shifting neighbouring cells.
ExcelDeleteRange.use_when: User wants to remove a sub-range and shift cells. To clear values use ClearRangeX.

ExcelFilterTable.description: Classic 'Filter Table' - apply or clear an AutoFilter on a named table.
ExcelFilterTable.use_when: User wants table filtering in classic projects. For StudioX use FilterX.

ExcelForEachRow.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

ExcelForEachRow.description: Classic 'For Each Row in Excel' - iterate the rows of a sheet/range from a classic Excel scope.
ExcelForEachRow.use_when: User wants row iteration in classic projects. For DataTable iteration use ForEachRow; for StudioX use ExcelForEachRowX (wizard-only).

ExcelGetCellColor.description: Classic 'Get Cell Color' - read the background colour of a cell into a Color variable.
ExcelGetCellColor.use_when: User needs the cell's colour value. For StudioX use GetCellColorX.

ExcelGetSelectedRange.description: Classic 'Get Selected Range' - read the currently selected range address into a string.
ExcelGetSelectedRange.use_when: User wants the user's current Excel selection. For StudioX use GetSelectedRangeX.

ExcelGetTableRange.description: Classic 'Get Table Range' - return the address of a named Excel table.
ExcelGetTableRange.use_when: User needs the address (e.g. A1:D20) of a named table for downstream activities.

ExcelGetWorkbookSheet.description: Classic 'Get Workbook Sheet' - return a worksheet by name or index from a workbook.
ExcelGetWorkbookSheet.use_when: User wants a sheet handle. For all sheet names use ExcelGetWorkbookSheets / GetSheets.

ExcelGetWorkbookSheets.description: Classic 'Get Workbook Sheets' - list the sheet names of the active workbook.
ExcelGetWorkbookSheets.use_when: User wants every sheet name for iteration or display. For StudioX use GetSheets.

ExcelInsertColumn.description: Classic 'Insert Column' - insert a column at a given position.
ExcelInsertColumn.use_when: User wants to add a column in classic projects. For StudioX use InsertColumnX.

ExcelInsertDeleteColumns.description: Classic 'Insert/Delete Columns' - insert or delete N columns in one call.
ExcelInsertDeleteColumns.use_when: User wants to add or drop multiple columns at once.

ExcelInsertDeleteRows.description: Classic 'Insert/Delete Rows' - insert or delete N rows in one call.
ExcelInsertDeleteRows.use_when: User wants to add or drop multiple rows at once.

ExcelLookUpRange.description: Classic 'Lookup Range' - find the address of a cell containing a given value within a range.
ExcelLookUpRange.use_when: User wants the cell address that holds a value. For VLOOKUP-style data lookup use VLookupX.

ExcelReadCell.description: Classic 'Read Cell' - read a single cell value into a string.
ExcelReadCell.use_when: User wants one cell's value in classic projects. For StudioX use ReadCellValueX or ReadCell.

ExcelReadCellFormula.description: Classic 'Read Cell Formula' - read the underlying formula text of a cell.
ExcelReadCellFormula.use_when: User wants the formula (=SUM(A1:A10)) rather than its evaluated value. For value use ExcelReadCell.

ExcelReadColumn.description: Classic 'Read Column' - read a single column starting at a cell into an IEnumerable<object>.
ExcelReadColumn.use_when: User wants one column of data without building a DataTable.

alternatives.use_instead_when: You need a rectangular range with multiple columns, not a single column of values.
You only need one cell from the column.

ExcelReadRange.description: Classic 'Read Range' - read a worksheet range into a DataTable from a classic Excel scope.
ExcelReadRange.use_when: User wants to read into a DataTable in classic projects. For StudioX use ReadRangeX.

ExcelReadRow.description: Classic 'Read Row' - read a single row starting at a cell into an IEnumerable<object>.
ExcelReadRow.use_when: User wants one row of data without building a DataTable.

ExcelRefreshPivotTable.description: Classic 'Refresh Pivot Table' - refresh a PivotTable after its source data changes.
ExcelRefreshPivotTable.use_when: User wants to refresh a PivotTable in classic projects. For StudioX use RefreshPivotTableX.

ExcelRemoveDuplicatesRange.description: Classic 'Remove Duplicates' - remove duplicate rows from a sheet range.
ExcelRemoveDuplicatesRange.use_when: User wants Excel to dedupe rows. For DataTable dedupe use RemoveDuplicateRows.

ExcelSaveWorkbook.description: Classic 'Save Workbook' - save the workbook of the surrounding ExcelApplicationScope.
ExcelSaveWorkbook.use_when: User wants to persist changes in classic projects.

ExcelSelectRange.description: Classic 'Select Range' - select a range so subsequent activities act on the user's selection.
ExcelSelectRange.use_when: User wants to set the active selection in classic projects. For StudioX use SelectRangeX.

ExcelSetRangeColor.description: Classic 'Set Range Color' - set the background colour of cells in a range.
ExcelSetRangeColor.use_when: User wants to colour cells in classic projects. For StudioX use FormatRangeX.

ExcelSortTable.description: Classic 'Sort Table' - sort a named Excel table by one or more columns.
ExcelSortTable.use_when: User wants table sorting in classic projects. For StudioX use SortX.

ExcelWriteCell.description: Classic 'Write Cell' - write a value or formula into a single cell.
ExcelWriteCell.use_when: User wants to set one cell in classic projects. For StudioX use WriteCellX.

ExcelWriteRange.description: Classic 'Write Range' - write a DataTable into a worksheet range from a classic Excel scope.
ExcelWriteRange.use_when: User wants to write a DataTable in classic projects. For StudioX use WriteRangeX; to append use ExcelAppendRange / AppendRangeX.

ExecuteMacro.description: Classic 'Execute Macro' - run a VBA macro defined in the active workbook with optional parameters.
ExecuteMacro.use_when: User wants to invoke an existing VBA macro. For StudioX use ExecuteMacroX. To run inline VBA code use InvokeVBA / InvokeVBAX.

ExecuteMacroX.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

ExecuteMacroX.description: StudioX 'Run Macro' - run a VBA macro defined in the active workbook with optional parameters.
ExecuteMacroX.use_when: User wants to invoke an existing VBA macro in a StudioX project. For inline VBA code use InvokeVBAX.

ExportExcelToCsvX.description: StudioX 'Export to CSV' - export a sheet of the active workbook to a CSV file.
ExportExcelToCsvX.use_when: User wants a CSV from an Excel sheet directly. For DataTable -> CSV use WriteCsvFile.

FillRangeX.description: StudioX 'Fill Range' - fill a range with a value or formula.
FillRangeX.use_when: User wants every cell in a range set to the same value/formula. For drag-fill behaviour use AutoFillX.

FindFirstLastDataRowX.description: StudioX activity that returns the first/last data row index in a sheet or table.
FindFirstLastDataRowX.use_when: User wants to compute where data starts/ends so they can append correctly.

FindReplaceValueX.description: StudioX 'Find/Replace Value' - search and replace values across a sheet, table, or range.
FindReplaceValueX.use_when: User wants Excel-native find/replace. For DataTable-side replace use FindAndReplace.

FormatRangeX.description: StudioX 'Format Range' - set font, alignment, borders, fill, and number format on a range.
FormatRangeX.use_when: User wants comprehensive cell formatting (not just colour). For colour-only use SetRangeColor / ExcelSetRangeColor.

GetCellColor.description: Modern 'Get Cell Color' - read the background colour of a cell.
GetCellColor.use_when: User wants the cell's colour value in modern projects. For classic use ExcelGetCellColor; for StudioX use GetCellColorX.

GetCellColorX.description: StudioX 'Get Cell Color' - read the background colour of a cell.
GetCellColorX.use_when: User wants the cell's colour value in StudioX. For classic use ExcelGetCellColor.

GetSelectedRangeX.description: StudioX 'Get Selected Range' - read the currently selected range address into a string.
GetSelectedRangeX.use_when: User wants the user's current Excel selection in StudioX. For classic use ExcelGetSelectedRange.

GetSensitivityLabelX.description: StudioX activity that reads the current Microsoft 365 sensitivity label of the active workbook.
GetSensitivityLabelX.use_when: User wants to read the workbook's sensitivity label. To set/replace one use AddSensitivityLabelX.

GetSheets.description: List the sheet names of the active workbook in StudioX/modern Excel.
GetSheets.use_when: User wants every sheet name for iteration. For classic use ExcelGetWorkbookSheets.

GetTableRange.description: Return the address (e.g. A1:D20) of a named Excel table.
GetTableRange.use_when: User needs a table's range address for downstream activities.

InsertColumnX.description: StudioX 'Insert Column' - insert a new column into a sheet or table.
InsertColumnX.use_when: User wants to add a column in StudioX. For classic use ExcelInsertColumn.

InsertRowsX.description: StudioX 'Insert Rows' - insert one or more rows into a sheet or table.
InsertRowsX.use_when: User wants to add rows at a position in StudioX.

InsertSheetX.description: StudioX 'Insert Sheet' - add a new worksheet to the active workbook.
InsertSheetX.use_when: User wants to add a new sheet. To clone an existing sheet use DuplicateSheetX.

InvokeVBA.description: Run inline VBA code against the active workbook (classic Excel projects).
InvokeVBA.use_when: User wants to execute custom VBA without saving a macro first. For StudioX use InvokeVBAX; for an existing macro use ExecuteMacro.

InvokeVBAX.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

InvokeVBAX.description: Run inline VBA code against the active workbook (StudioX Excel projects).
InvokeVBAX.use_when: User wants to execute custom VBA inline in StudioX. For an existing named macro use ExecuteMacroX.

LookupX.description: StudioX 'Lookup' - find a value in a range and return the corresponding cell address.
LookupX.use_when: User wants the address of a found value. For VLOOKUP-style data return use VLookupX.

MatchFunctionX.description: StudioX 'Match' - return the position (index) of a value within a range.
MatchFunctionX.use_when: User wants the relative index of a matched value (Excel MATCH semantics).

OpenWorkbook.description: Open an existing .xlsx workbook on disk and return a workbook variable.
OpenWorkbook.use_when: User wants to load an existing workbook. To create a new one use CreateNewWorkbook; to use a scope-style container use ExcelApplicationScope or ExcelApplicationCard.

ProtectSheetX.description: StudioX 'Protect Sheet' - apply password protection to a worksheet.
ProtectSheetX.use_when: User wants to lock a sheet against edits. To remove protection use UnprotectSheetX.

ReadCell.description: Modern 'Read Cell' - read a single cell value (string) using the active Excel scope.
ReadCell.use_when: User wants a single cell's text in modern Excel. For typed value use ReadCellValueX; for formula use ReadCellFormulaX.

alternatives.use_instead_when: You need more than a single cell; ReadRange returns a rectangular block as a DataTable.
The workflow uses the modern Excel (X) suite rather than the classic Workbook activities.

ReadCellFormula.description: Modern 'Read Cell Formula' - read the formula text of a cell.
ReadCellFormula.use_when: User wants the formula not the result. For just the value use ReadCell or ReadCellValueX.

ReadCellFormulaX.description: StudioX 'Read Cell Formula' - read the formula text of a cell.
ReadCellFormulaX.use_when: User wants the formula (=SUM(A1:A10)) rather than its evaluated value. For just the value use ReadCellValueX or ReadCell.

ReadCellValueX.description: StudioX 'Read Cell Value' - read a single cell value (typed) using the active Excel scope.
ReadCellValueX.use_when: User wants one cell's typed value in StudioX. For string-only use ReadCell; for formula text use ReadCellFormulaX.

ReadColumn.description: Modern 'Read Column' - read one column of values into an IEnumerable<object>.
ReadColumn.use_when: User wants a single column of data. For a row use ReadRow; for full range use ReadRangeX.

ReadRow.description: Modern 'Read Row' - read one row of values into an IEnumerable<object>.
ReadRow.use_when: User wants a single row. For a column use ReadColumn; for a full range use ReadRangeX.

RefreshDataConnectionsX.description: StudioX activity that refreshes all (or named) data connections in the active workbook.
RefreshDataConnectionsX.use_when: User wants to pull fresh data from external connections (Power Query, ODBC). For PivotTables alone use RefreshPivotTableX.

RefreshPivotTableX.description: StudioX 'Refresh Pivot Table' - refresh a PivotTable after its source data changes.
RefreshPivotTableX.use_when: User wants to refresh a single PivotTable. For all data connections use RefreshDataConnectionsX.

RemoveDuplicatesX.description: StudioX 'Remove Duplicates' - remove duplicate rows from a sheet range or table.
RemoveDuplicatesX.use_when: User wants in-place dedupe in StudioX. For classic use ExcelRemoveDuplicatesRange; for DataTable dedupe use RemoveDuplicateRows.

RenameSheetX.description: StudioX 'Rename Sheet' - rename an existing worksheet.
RenameSheetX.use_when: User wants to rename a sheet in StudioX.

SaveAsPdfX.description: StudioX 'Save as PDF' - export the workbook (or a sheet/range) to a PDF file.
SaveAsPdfX.use_when: User wants a PDF output of an Excel file. To save as a different Excel file use SaveExcelFileAsX.

SaveExcelFileAsX.description: StudioX 'Save Excel File As' - save the active workbook to a new path.
SaveExcelFileAsX.use_when: User wants to save under a different filename or folder. For in-place save use SaveExcelFileX.

SaveExcelFileX.description: StudioX 'Save Excel File' - save the active workbook to its existing path.
SaveExcelFileX.use_when: User wants to persist changes in place. To save as a new file use SaveExcelFileAsX.

SelectRangeX.description: StudioX 'Select Range' - set the active selection in a sheet so subsequent activities act on it.
SelectRangeX.use_when: User wants to mark a range as the active selection.

SequenceX.description: StudioX Sequence container that groups Excel-related child activities under a logical step.
SequenceX.use_when: User wants to group StudioX Excel steps in a labelled container.

SetRangeColor.description: Modern 'Set Range Color' - set the background colour of cells in a range.
SetRangeColor.use_when: User wants to colour cells in modern projects. For wider formatting use FormatRangeX.

SortX.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

SortX.description: StudioX 'Sort' - sort a sheet range or table by one or more columns.
SortX.use_when: User wants Excel-side sorting. For classic table sort use ExcelSortTable.

TextToColumnsX.description: StudioX 'Text to Columns' - split a column by a delimiter into multiple columns.
TextToColumnsX.use_when: User wants Excel's Text-to-Columns feature applied to a range.

UnprotectSheetX.description: StudioX 'Unprotect Sheet' - remove password protection from a worksheet.
UnprotectSheetX.use_when: User wants to unlock a previously protected sheet. To protect use ProtectSheetX.

VLookupX.description: StudioX 'VLookup' - perform an Excel-style VLOOKUP against a range and return the matched value.
VLookupX.use_when: User wants VLOOKUP semantics. For arbitrary cell-address lookup use ExcelLookUpRange / LookupX; for general matching use MatchFunctionX.

WithWorkbook.Body.content: ActivityAction x:TypeArguments='x:Object' with DelegateInArgument WSSessionData and Sequence Do body

WithWorkbook.description: Project Notebook scope that opens a workbook and runs nested activities against it (StudioX project notebook).
WithWorkbook.use_when: User is using the StudioX Project Notebook pattern and wants the implicit workbook scope.

WriteCellX.description: StudioX 'Write Cell' - write a value or formula into a single cell.
WriteCellX.use_when: User wants to set one cell in StudioX. For classic use ExcelWriteCell.

alternatives.use_instead_when: You are using the classic Workbook activities rather than the modern Excel (X) suite.
You are writing a DataTable or multiple cells rather than a single value.

WriteCsvFile.description: Write a DataTable to a CSV file (overwriting any existing file).
WriteCsvFile.use_when: User wants a CSV from a DataTable. To grow an existing CSV use AppendCsvFile.
